title: From<Option<T>> removed from Patch<T>
description: Patch<T> previously implemented From<Option<T>>, mapping None to Patch::Keep and Some(v) to Patch::Set(v). This is semantically wrong: None from user-space often means clear this nullable field, not leave it unchanged. The two are distinct behaviors (omit the key vs. send null). With From<Option<T>> removed, callers must choose Patch::Keep, Patch::Clear, or Patch::Set(v) explicitly. A contributor might suggest adding it back for convenience. Do not: the wrong default (Keep instead of Clear on None) causes silent data loss on fields the user intended to clear.
close_reason: Decision recorded during idfq epic implementation; design intentionally retained as-is.
